items.title: IASB Faces Leadership Transition as Barckow's Term Ends
items.summary: Andreas Barckow's five-year term as IASB Chair concludes on 30 June 2026, and the IFRS Foundation has yet to announce a permanent successor. Vice-Chair Linda Mezon-Hutter will serve as Acting Chair while the search continues, an unusual gap for the board that sets global accounting standards.

items.title: FRC Clarifies Auditor Duties Under Provision 29
items.summary: With the UK Corporate Governance Code's Provision 29 now applying to financial years beginning on or after 1 January 2026, the FRC has set out what auditors are and are not required to do when a company's annual report includes a board declaration on material controls. The guidance accompanies the FRC's revised ISA (UK) 700, 701 and 720 reporting standards, effective from 15 December 2026.

items.title: FRC Modernises Audit Enforcement Procedure
items.summary: The FRC has overhauled its Audit Enforcement Procedure, moving to a more integrated regulatory model that links supervision, investigation and enforcement so problems are spotted earlier and resolved faster. The reform is meant to give the UK audit market quicker outcomes and clearer lessons learned from enforcement cases.

items.title: PCAOB Proposes Easing Quality Control Standard
items.summary: The PCAOB has proposed amendments to its QC 1000 quality control standard that would remove the external quality control function and design-only compliance requirements, responding to pushback from audit firms over implementation burden. The proposal is open for public comment until 9 July 2026.

items.title: Big Four Scale Up AI Agents Across Audit and Tax
items.summary: Deloitte, PwC, KPMG and EY are moving AI from pilot projects to scaled deployment, with EY rolling out AI agents across its tax practice and KPMG continuing a multi-year, multi-billion-dollar AI investment programme. The shift is also reshaping hiring, with AI-related roles now outpacing traditional audit postings across the firms.

items.title: Demand for Sustainability Assurance Keeps Climbing
items.summary: A growing share of large global companies are now seeking third-party assurance on their sustainability reporting, with audit firms picking up more of that work as ISSB-aligned standards roll out across more than 35 jurisdictions. New assurance and ethics standards from the IAASB and IESBA are helping firms respond to the rising demand.
